question_text: Let {−1, − 2 , 1, 2 , 3} be a realization of a random sample of size 5 from a population having 𝑁( 2 , 𝜎2) distribution, where 𝜎> 0 is an unknown parameter. Let 𝑇 be an unbiased estimator of 𝜎2 whose variance attains the

options.C: ramer-Rao lower bound. Then based on the above data, the realized value of 𝑇 (rounded off to two decimal places) equals _______________
